response_format.description: All responses must follow this structured format

example.name: Chia Seeds
example.history: Chia (Salvia hispanica) was a staple food of the Aztecs and Mayans dating back to 3500 BC. The word 'chia' comes from the Mayan word for 'strength.'

nutrition_profile.notable_properties: Hydrophilic — absorbs up to 12x its weight in water. Gluten-free.

example.best_time_to_eat: Morning (breakfast) is ideal for sustained energy and satiety.

suggestions: Chia Pudding: Mix 2 tbsp with 1/2 cup milk, refrigerate overnight
Smoothies: Add 1 tbsp for extra fiber and omega-3s
Baking: Use as egg substitute (1 tbsp chia + 3 tbsp water = 1 egg)
additional_notes: Soak chia seeds for at least 15 minutes before eating to aid digestion and maximize nutrient absorption.
Chia seeds are high in omega-3 ALA, but conversion to EPA/DHA is limited — consider algae-based supplements for DHA.
Start with 1 tbsp/day if new to chia seeds to avoid digestive discomfort from the high fiber content.

recipe.name: Overnight Chia Pudding
recipe.description: A simple, no-cook vegan breakfast packed with omega-3s and fiber.
instructions: Mix chia seeds, almond milk, vanilla, and maple syrup in a jar.
Stir well and refrigerate overnight (at least 4 hours).
Stir again before serving, top with fresh berries.
